body_excerpt:
1\. Connected endpoints purged 🐞 Bug ≡ Correctness 
MultiNodeSubscription.RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints() currently flags endpoints for removal when
IsSubscriberConnected is true, so UpdateSubscriptions() will purge healthy connections from the
tracked server set. This breaks multi-node subscription bookkeeping (used for keyspace
notifications) and can trigger repeated (un)subscribe churn and incorrect subscription state.
Agent Prompt ``` ## Issue description `MultiNodeSubscription.RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints()` is intended to remove *disconnected* subscriber endpoints from `_servers`, but it currently removes endpoints that are still connected (`IsSubscriberConnected == true`). This causes `RedisSubscriber.UpdateSubscriptions()` to drop healthy endpoints from multi-node subscriptions, breaking the internal model of where the client is subscribed. ## Issue Context - `RedisSubscriber.UpdateSubscriptions()` calls `RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints()` for every subscription. - `SingleNodeSubscription.RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints()` correctly clears endpoints only when `IsSubscriberConnected` is false; multi-node should follow the same semantics. ## Fix Focus Areas - src/StackExchange.Redis/Subscription.cs[386-420] - src/StackExchange.Redis/RedisSubscriber.cs[115-124] - src/StackExchange.Redis/Subscription.cs[261-271] ### Proposed change - In `MultiNodeSubscription.RemoveDisconnectedEndpoints()`, change: - `if (server.Value.IsSubscriberConnected)` - to `if (!server.Value.IsSubscriberConnected)` - Keep the rest of the scratch-buffer removal logic intact. - (Optional) Consider returning the pooled `EndPoint[]` with clearing to avoid retaining endpoint references in the pool, if that aligns with project memory-retention policy. ``` ⓘ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools
